name: Ravioli Filled W/Pumpkin
recipeInstructions:
For pasta: Combine flours and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a food processor. Add eggs, olive oil, and water and blend until dough comes together, about 20 seconds. Turn dough out onto lightly floured surface and knead until smooth, about 3 minutes. Wrap in plastic wrap. Let stand 15 minutes.
For filling: Process the pumpkin, Parmesan cheese, amaretto, eggs, salt, white pepper, nutmeg, and olive oil until a smooth mixture forms. Set aside.
To assemble: Cut dough into 6 pieces. Flatten one piece of dough (keep the rest covered to prevent drying), then fold it into thirds. Run through a pasta machine on the widest setting, folding before each run and dusting with flour if sticky. Gradually narrow the settings on the pasta machine, running the dough through without folding until it's about 1/16 inch thick, dusting with flour as needed. Repeat with another piece of dough. Place one sheet of dough on a lightly floured surface. Mound small spoonfuls of filling at 2-inch intervals, leaving a half-inch border along the edges. Brush the dough with water between the filling and along the edges. Top with the second sheet of dough, pressing around the filling to seal. Trim around the filling using a pastry cutter to create ravioli. Arrange on lightly floured waxed paper. Repeat with remaining dough. Let ravioli dry until leathery around the edges. (Can be prepared up to 1 day ahead and refrigerated.)
Stir 2 tablespoons olive oil into boiling salted water in a large pot. Add ravioli (in batches if necessary) and cook until just tender but firm to bite, about 8 minutes. Drain well. Transfer to a bowl. Add butter and toss well. Divide among 8 plates. Sprinkle with freshly grated Parmesan cheese if desired and top with freshly ground pepper.
